title: Bisect, Reproducible Diagnosis, and Automated Regression Search · Repository Trace: Gita Bisect Skip Flaky Merge
summary: A nondeterministic performance regression lies within 2,400 commits, skips unbuildable revisions, depends on generated assets, and may have entered on a merged side branch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita bisect skip flaky merge increment in reproducible bisect harness and causal report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Handle untestable commits, flaky observations, merge topology, multiple introduction paths, and inconclusive adjacent candidates honestly.
Build a hermetic bisect run harness with explicit exit meanings, timeouts, cleanup, logs, deterministic fixtures, and no unsafe repository code execution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Bisect Skip Flaky Merge
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G71 is a theory in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe.

The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe. content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 1
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set
git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts
# expected: bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity
# observation: the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID
# changed-case: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe
# transfer-limit: execute real historical code only in an isolated environment with bounded credentials and network

# Evidence: gita-bisect-skip-flaky-merge-hr0g9v
# scenario-key: trace-hr0g9v probe-v9g0rh boundary-0g9vhr owner-hr0g9vx fault-hr0g9vz

# hypothesis: the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set
git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts
# expected: bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity
# observation: the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID
# changed-case: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly
# after-ref: main=a11cehr candidate=c0ffeev

# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
